description: Persistent issue tracker. Decider appends new issues from Curmudgeon reviews, marks resolved when wins.json no longer contains the flagged text.

issues.description: External report #1 (Devilwench): Positive feedback only. No error claim, correction, or scientific content. "You're awesome don't ever change." No changes needed.
issues.fix_details: Not a problem report — positive feedback only. Analyst logged as reader engagement data point.

issues.description: EXP-060 PIPELINE: generate-html.js needs support for kill-shots.json data file to resolve {{KS_TOTAL}}, {{KS_STRAW_MAN_COUNT}}, {{KS_WAIT_AND_SEE_COUNT}} template placeholders in Part 5 HTML. data/kill-shots.json already created (6 entries). EXP-060 replacement_html uses these placeholders but they are currently unresolved. Current Part 5 uses EXP-057 prose (hardcoded). When pipeline supports it, swap EXP-057 HTML for EXP-060 HTML to get data-driven count injection.
issues.notes: Not urgent — existing Part 5 HTML is complete and correct via EXP-057. This is a refactoring to data-driven counts. Lower priority than content work.

issues.title: predictions-catalog: build schema mismatch — PRED_STD_RELABEL uses derivation field that does not exist
issues.description: generate-html.js counts stdRelabel via entries.filter(e => e.derivation === "standard_physics"), but no entry has a derivation field. Same for testability, test_window.status. These four build placeholders (PRED_STD_RELABEL, PRED_TESTABLE, PRED_DOME_DERIVED, PRED_ACTIVE_WINDOWS) are permanently 0. Not currently rendered in section HTML but noted in _meta as ISS-730. May conflict with ISS-730 — check.

issues.title: Section 2.9 content absent from DOCX/PDF (build-doc-v4.js gap)
issues.description: Section 2.9 (Finsler Distance Formula) was comprehensively rewritten by EXP-003/027/042 with strong content, but build-doc-v4.js does not include this section in the DOCX/PDF output. The full content (V13 formula, MDS circularity, globe fingerprints, Singapore derivation) is missing from the downloadable document formats. Infrastructure fix needed in build-doc-v4.js.
issues.suggested_fix: Audit build-doc-v4.js to find why part2/SEC-2.9 content is excluded. Add the section to the DOCX build pipeline. Likely needs a new section-rendering block for SEC-2.9.

issues.title: EXP-057-proposal: our_strength field (decisive/major/supportive) undefined — missing formal definitions
issues.description: EXP-057 introduces our_strength field in kill-shots but lacks formal definitions for decisive/major/supportive ratings. Without definitions, assignments appear arbitrary and dome defender can challenge on a win-by-win basis. Source: curmudgeon EXP-057-proposal.c2.json.
issues.suggested_fix: Analyst must define our_strength criteria before EXP-057 is integrated. Add definitions to DATA-SCHEMAS.md and to the kill-shots tab explanation.

issues.description: predictions.json: 12 entries have restates_win set (pointing to a specific WIN) but our_verdict is NOT recycled. Verdicts observed: pending, standard_physics, unfalsifiable. Analyst should audit and either change to recycled or document justification.

SEC-6.1 attacks WIN-053 formula B(r) = 62,376×exp(-r/8619) + 64,852×exp(-r/8619) as a developmental/preliminary version. Dome model page may use simpler B(r) = 60,000×exp(-r/8619) single-pole form as primary published claim (OPEN-011). Review SEC-6.1-params.c2 hole 2: coefficients may belong to a two-pole form listed as under development.
issues.notes: Need to verify which formula version is the primary published claim on dome model page vs wins page. If 60,000 is the current published formula, the attack needs to shift to target that single-pole form. Also: the two terms in the two-pole form have DIFFERENT arguments (r_N vs r_S = R - r_N) so the simplification to 127,228 is incorrect regardless.

issues.description: HOL-TAXONOMY (cycle 1): Inconsistent boundary between Misleading and Std Model Explains verdict categories. Seven Std Model WINs use "Non-discriminating" language in findings (WIN-006, 010, 020, 035, 036, 040, 060) while several Misleading WINs make essentially the same argument. Evaluation Guide needs clearer decision criterion distinguishing the two categories.

SEC-2b.7 W001 paragraph cites sigma_distance 7.3 relative to ≈0.8 nT prediction uncertainty, but the source of the 0.8 nT figure is not given. The dome's results.json sigma_distance is calculated from (obs - pred) / sigma_pred, where sigma_pred is the dome's own prediction precision. Our text should cite the exact dome-file field where this denominator comes from to prevent the dome saying our sigma calculation is wrong.

issues.title: HOL-NARRATIVE: Overview scorecard declares 0 Actual Wins before presenting evidence
issues.description: HOL-NARRATIVE cycle 1: The Overview scorecard declares "0 Actual Wins" before the reader has seen any evidence. A dome defender will immediately object: "They decided the verdict before the trial." The scorecard is a conclusion, not an introduction. Consider restructuring to present evidence first (brief summary of methodology) before the verdict, or reframe the scorecard as "our interim finding after reviewing all claims" with a direct link to the Methodology tab.

issues.title: HOL-STRESSTEST: No explicit success category defined — what would a genuine dome WIN look like?
issues.description: HOL-STRESSTEST cycle 1: The review defines what disqualifies a WIN but never operationalizes what WOULD constitute a genuine win. If the dome model produced a genuinely discriminating prediction, would we recognize it? The Evaluation Guide defines the standard, but no WIN has ever been shown to meet it, and we never explicitly tell the reader what evidence would change our verdict. This makes the review vulnerable to "moving the goalposts" accusations.

issues.title: HOL-COMPLETENESS: Part 4.6 (SEC-2b) code analysis gives aggregate stats but zero specific WIN examples
issues.description: HOL-COMPLETENESS cycle 1: Part 4.6 presents statistics (24 hardcoded, 34 no validation, 6 live-fetch) but never names a single WIN as illustration. A dome defender asks: "Show me one." We cannot without a lookup. Every aggregate claim should be backed by at least one named example: e.g., "WIN-031 has hardcoded value 97.0% for the passing rate" or "WIN-007 has no monitoring code at all." Source: curmudgeon HOL-COMPLETENESS.json.

issues.title: HOL-REDUNDANCY: Eclipse/Chapman argument restated in 6+ separate locations with inconsistent detail
issues.description: HOL-REDUNDANCY cycle 1: The eclipse/Chapman argument (that the dome's eclipse prediction is explained by the Chapman mechanism) appears in at least 6 places: WIN-036-038, Section 6.1, Section 4.5, and kill-shot tests. Each repetition has slightly different detail and phrasing, creating both bloat and inconsistency risk. A definitive treatment should live in one canonical location (probably Section 6.x or the kill-shots) with brief cross-references elsewhere.

issues.title: HOL-STRESSTEST: Std Model Explains (24%) conflates two distinct situations — globe-compatible vs globe-predicted
issues.description: HOL-STRESSTEST hole 1: The "Std Model Explains" verdict conflates: (a) dome prediction matches observation AND matches standard physics prediction (dome and globe predict same — no discrimination possible), and (b) dome prediction matches observation but standard physics ALSO explains it via a different mechanism (dome is compatible but not uniquely supported). These are different epistemic situations and should be clearly distinguished to avoid a defender claiming our taxonomy is unfair.

SEC-6.1 labels the B(r) = 62,376×exp(−r/8619) + 64,852×exp(−r/8619) formula as WIN-012 (Geomagnetic field) but WIN-012 is the mag-gravity coupling constant κ = 1.67 nT/μGal. The B(r) formula belongs to WIN-053. This is a WIN misattribution in section prose.

HOL-HIERARCHY cycle 1: No organizing principle for parts 3-5. Statistical analysis (Part 4.6) buried after all rebuttals. Kill-shots appear before predictions with no framing on why they represent higher-tier evidence. The hierarchical logic is never stated explicitly.

KILLSHOT-GAIA cycle 1: Missing explicit error analysis. Section asserts dome fails Gaia parallax but does not compute expected dome parallax from its geometry and compare to Gaia catalog values. Also missing: why Gaia internal consistency is incompatible with flat-disc geometry (solar system parallax would be entirely wrong).

EXEC-SUMMARY cycle 1: Declares 0 Actual Wins upfront with no preemption of obvious dome-defender objection ("you're using globe assumptions to evaluate a globe-alternative model"). Missing: 1-sentence anticipation of this objection. Action: no_change recommended by curmudgeon but 7 major holes found.

SEC-4.1 (9.2-Sigma dashboard) bottom line contradicts table entry for Polaris. Bottom line says globe and dome predict the same for Polaris, but the table row shows a different classification. This is a self-contradiction in the section. Source: SEC-3.1.json (now part4, SEC-4.1).

issues.title: SEC-1.8: n(r)/Gaia argument missing chromaticity bridge — dome can argue systematic offset
issues.description: The n(r)/Gaia argument in Section 1.8 is vulnerable to: "If n(r) bends ALL light uniformly, Gaia sees self-consistent positions even though they differ from true positions — differential measurements are unchanged." The fatal counter (chromatic dispersion: n varies with wavelength, but Gaia detects wavelength-dependent color shifts that ARE NOT observed) is not in the section. Without the chromaticity bridge, a dome defender can dismiss the entire Gaia kill-shot. Source: SEC-1.8.c1.json hole 3.

issues.title: predictions.json: 86 entries all have our_verdict=null — recycling/standard_physics detection non-functional
issues.description: Curmudgeon ISS-703-sha256-forensics review found that predictions.json currently has our_verdict=null for all 86 entries. The recycling detection logic (comparing predictions to WIN verdicts) cannot function without verdicts. The predictions tab analysis claims to show how many predictions are recycled, standard physics, etc. — but these categories are all 0. This is a major data gap. Analyst must audit all predictions, apply our_verdict values (recycled/standard_physics/unfalsifiable/pending/etc.) using the established verdict framework. Source: ISS-703-sha256-forensics digest entry.

issues.title: EXHIBIT-AB: Evidence Exhibits A & B have no analysis — dome's most prominent home-page feature unaddressed
issues.description: Curmudgeon EXHIBIT-AB review found a major coverage gap: Exhibits A and B (the dome's correlation charts for "Aetheric Influence Index" and magnetic field measurements) are the most prominent feature on the dome's home page, but our review has no dedicated section addressing them. A visitor seeing the site for the first time sees these charts. The statistical claims (r = 0.992, AI = 1.152) are unaddressed. This is our biggest blind spot — a dome defender can point to Exhibits A/B as independent validation and we have nothing to say. Analyst must write a dedicated analysis section. Source: EXHIBIT-AB digest entry.

issues.title: SEC-2.1: Arithmetic error in equatorial cavity height calculation
issues.description: Curmudgeon SEC-2.1 digest review found an arithmetic error in the equatorial height calculation. The section states H(r) at r=15,000 km but the computation is wrong. Analyst must verify the H(r) formula with correct values, fix the arithmetic, and ensure the stated conclusion (8.7 Hz minimum) follows from corrected numbers. Source: SEC-2.1 digest entry, action: major_rewrite.

issues.title: SEC-4.5.4: Wrong sun altitude value in HTML section — states 8,537 km (apparent) not physical
issues.description: Curmudgeon SEC-4.5.4 digest review found the HTML section uses the wrong sun altitude. The section says "A local sun at fixed height H ≈ 8,537 km" but 8,537 km is the apparent altitude. The dome model assigns the sun a different physical altitude (< 4,200 km per the refraction cascade). The correct value to use depends on whether the section is analyzing apparent or physical coordinates, and the text is ambiguous/wrong. Analyst must correct. Source: SEC-4.5.4 digest entry.

issues.title: WIN-058b: Monte Carlo range r = 0.44-0.69 is likely too low — needs SNR-aware simulation
issues.description: WIN-058b claims Monte Carlo simulation gives r = 0.44-0.69 for shared storm envelope. Curmudgeon quantitative verification: at SNR=3.3 (G5 storm level), expected r ~ 0.91. The stated range implies noise-dominated signals. A dome defender could use our own simulation to argue the excess correlation proves kappa. Analyst must re-derive with appropriate G5-storm SNR parameters. Source: WIN-058b.c1.json.

issues.title: SEC-6.4: Internal latitude contradiction between WIN-024 evidence and SEC-6.4 prose
issues.description: SEC-6.4 (part6-rewrite-EXP067.json c2 review): INTERNAL LATITUDE CONTRADICTION between WIN-024 detail_evidence and SEC-6.4 text. WIN-024 detail_evidence states 'the standard IGRF/WMM reference places the SAA southern boundary at ~40-45°S geographic latitude.' SEC-6.4 prose uses a different latitude range. These must be aligned. Source: part6-rewrite-EXP067.json.

issues.title: SEC-2b.1: TLDR overgeneralizes adaptive tolerance + self-contradicted WIN count wrong
issues.description: SEC-2b.1 (SEC-2b.1.c2.json): (1) TLDR says 'even those use adaptive tolerances with 50% error floors' but only one of the 4 live-fetch WINs has been shown to use this tolerance. Change TLDR to reference only documented cases. (2) Stratification paragraph incorrectly lists 9 self-contradicted WINs as unmonitored; the actual count should exclude WINs already having monitoring domains. Source: SEC-2b.1.c2.json.

issues.title: SEC-2b.7: Causal inference overstated + scoring criterion needs citation
issues.description: SEC-2b.7 (SEC-2b.7-rewrite.c2.json): (1) Paragraph states scoreboard was 'removed approximately one day after our poller flagged its drift' — this conflates correlation with causation. Separate factual claim (endpoint returns 404) from causal inference. (2) The 'direction_correct AND snr_sufficient' scoring criterion is stated as fact without citation from the dome's code. Source: SEC-2b.7-rewrite.c2.json.

issues.title: SEC-3.2: Wrong prediction description + ignores E-PRED-A through E-PRED-D model tests
issues.description: SEC-3.2 (SEC-3.2.json): (1) The -17 to -21 nT prediction range is oversimplified; dome has a dual-baseline per-station system (9 per-station predictions). (2) Section completely ignores dome's four model-discriminating tests E-PRED-A through E-PRED-D; E-PRED-B (Hartland FSF-based relative prediction) is particularly significant. Source: SEC-3.2.json.

issues.title: SEC-3.3: Rule 15 citation unverifiable from archived V50.6 raw text
issues.description: SEC-3.3 (SEC-3.3.json): Test 3 cites 'Rule 15: Say aetheric slipstreams NOT jet streams' from the AI Context page. However V50.6 raw-text extraction lists Rule 15 differently. Citation must be verified against archived raw text or updated. Source: SEC-3.3.json.

issues.title: SEC-3.5.7: Wrong prediction description + ignores E-PRED model tests
issues.description: SEC-3.5.7 (SEC-3.5.7.json): Same issues as SEC-3.2 — (1) -17 to -21 nT range is oversimplified for the dome's dual-baseline per-station prediction system. (2) Section ignores E-PRED-A through E-PRED-D discriminating tests. E-PRED-B specifically predicts Hartland (80% coverage, FSF 1.471) will show stronger signal than Ebro, which the Chapman mechanism also predicts differently. Source: SEC-3.5.7.json.

issues.title: SEC-4.5.3: HTML/DOCX section numbering mismatch + HTML lacks key quantitative content
issues.description: SEC-4.5.3 (SEC-4.5.3.json): (1) HTML section 4.5.3 is 'Gravity at the Rim: 90% Drop' but DOCX section 4.5.3 is 'Solar Angular Diameter: 29x Too Much Variation' — same number, different content. Sections are misaligned between HTML and DOCX. (2) HTML section 4.5.3 lacks the H(r) quantitative calculation that the DOCX version includes (H(pole)=8537km, H(rim)=837km, 152x ratio). Source: SEC-4.5.3.json.

issues.title: SEC-5.14/5.15/5.16: Sections exist in HTML but are entirely absent from DOCX/PDF build
issues.description: SEC-5.14 (SEC-5.14.json) + SEC-5.15 (SEC-5.15.json) + SEC-5.16 (SEC-5.16.json): Sections 5.14, 5.15, and 5.16 exist in generate-html.js but are entirely absent from build-doc-v4.js. The DOCX/PDF skips from 5.13 to Part 6. These sections include some of the review's strongest original arguments about dome author's own AI-context engineering. Must add to DOCX builder. Third curmudgeon flag on this gap. Source: SEC-5.14/5.15/5.16 reviews.

issues.title: DOCX hardcodes Self-Contradicted count as 11 but actual count is now 12
issues.description: SEC-6.1.json DOCX check: build-doc-v4.js hardcodes the Self-Contradicted count as '11' in Pattern 11 and the 6.3 final tally, but the actual count from wins.json is now 12 (WIN-068 added via EXP-078 Refraction Cascade). CLAUDE.md explicitly prohibits hardcoding counts derivable from data. Must replace hardcoded '11' with dynamic count. Source: SEC-6.1.json.

issues.title: SEC-6.2: Provenance argument needs stronger mathematical framing
issues.description: SEC-6.2 (SEC-6.2.c2.json): Section frames the argument as '40,030 km is suspiciously close to 40,075 km — Earth's equatorial circumference.' A dome defender can say 'those are 45 km apart, that's not suspicious.' The stronger argument leads with the 2πR relationship: 40,030 = 2π × 6,381.7 km to within 0.06%, which is well within the dome's own stated measurement precision. The dome has converged on a globe-derived number to within its own uncertainty. Source: SEC-6.2.c2.json.

SEC-4.8 (KILLSHOT-GPS.json c1 + SEC-4.8.json c1) BATCH: (1) Self-contradictory numbers: section says sun appears ~50% larger at noon but also says variation is ~±1.7% (matching observations) — these cannot both be true for the same claim; (2) Aetheric refraction defense never addressed in Section 4.8 despite being the dome's standard escape hatch; (3) Heavy redundancy with Section 4.5.4 (Solar Diameter: 50% Variation) — sections are near-duplicate and should be merged or clearly differentiated. Needs major rewrite. Source: SEC-4.8.json cycle 1.

WIN-002.c2: The dome model explicitly addresses the WIN-002/WIN-029 tension in its evolution text (06-evolution.txt). WIN-002 claims 26% aetheric damping matches Schumann resonance. WIN-029 uses a different resonant cavity size. The dome's own evolution log explains this 'reconciliation' via the H(r) curve. Our review does not engage with this dome explanation — a defender who cites the evolution text can claim we ignored the dome's own documented reconciliation. Source: WIN-002.c2.json.

KILLSHOT-GPS (GPS kill-shot section): The +38.3 μs/day figure (gravitational + velocity relativistic correction combined) needs explicit sourcing. Text says this comes from GR predictions but does not cite the primary source (e.g., GPS ICD or Ashby 2002 Liv. Rev. Rel.). A hostile reader can claim this is a round number or approximation and demand the calculation. Source: KILLSHOT-GPS.json cycle 1.

KILLSHOT-SH (Southern Hemisphere kill-shot): The SYD-EZE V12 error claim of '-78%' is stated but the derivation is not shown inline. A dome defender can challenge the number and we have no inline calculation to point to. The derivation should be shown or cited to our own data files. Source: KILLSHOT-SH.json cycle 1.

KILLSHOT-SH (Southern Hemisphere kill-shot): The 126,000 km Antarctic circumnavigation argument (flat-earth disc circumference cannot be reconciled with observed flight times) is mentioned in CLAUDE.md and SESSION-CONTEXT as a key kill-shot but is absent from the kill-shot section. This is one of the most concrete, easily-verified arguments against the dome model. Should be added to the section. Source: KILLSHOT-SH.json cycle 1.

EXP-054-batch.c2: V-template mismatch for Unfalsifiable/theological WINs (WIN-023, WIN-032, WIN-034). The batch audit appended sentences using the V-template which opens with 'The dome's original test was...' but unfalsifiable WINs have no testable prediction to reference. The template sentence is logically incoherent for these WIN types. The appended sentences for unfalsifiable WINs need a different template. Source: EXP-054-batch.c2.json.

EVAL-GUIDE (Evaluation Guide section): Principle 3 claims 'Mainstream physics is not assumed correct by default' but the evaluation methodology uses mainstream-derived measurements as the reference standard for all comparisons. The guide cannot simultaneously claim neutrality on mainstream physics and then compare dome predictions against NOAA magnetometry, Gaia astrometry, and GPS timing. The principle should be reworded to: 'We do not assume the dome's predictions are wrong simply because they differ from mainstream cosmology' — a more limited and accurate claim. Source: EVAL-GUIDE.json cycle 1.

FAIL-004 (SEC-6.x): The equivalence claim '≥+1.5σ threshold that would correspond to a ≥3% anomaly' conflates AAO index sigma (standardized anomaly) with the Roaring Forties wind percentage. The AAO index σ unit is not directly proportional to a percentage wind anomaly. The cited ≥3% threshold for the dome's prediction applies to wind speed/pressure, not to AAO index units. Either show the conversion explicitly or remove the threshold equivalence. Source: part6-FAIL-004.c2.json.

FAIL-004 (SEC-6.x): No citation for the +0.78σ AAO value stated as the outcome during the prediction window. The text mentions 'NCEP AAO data' but the specific +0.78σ value lacks a DOI, dataset URL, or data archive reference. Should cite the NOAA Climate Prediction Center AAO data archive with the specific file/date range used. Source: part6-FAIL-004.c2.json.

SEC-6.3 (old SEC-3.5.3 — dome parallel-plate Schumann critique): Section asserts 'the dome's parallel-plate cavity geometry actually predicts Schumann frequencies at X Hz' but does not show the derivation. A dome defender or reader cannot verify the predicted frequency without seeing the calculation using dome parameters (firmament height 8,537 km). Should add a footnote showing f = c/(2H) for a parallel-plate cavity using H from the dome's own parameters. Source: SEC-3.5.3.json cycle 1.

EXP-050-r2: The analyst's revision 2 for EXP-050 still proposes flipping WIN-018 from its current verdict, despite the cycle 1 curmudgeon review rejecting this flip. The argument about 'effective solar orbit radius' used to justify the flip is vague and could mislead section prose. Before accepting this modification, the WIN-018 flip must be explicitly gate-2 adjudicated. Source: EXP-050-r2.json cycle 2.

issues.title: PRED-TOROID-003 (Schumann Δf ≈ 0.336 Hz) is unclassified but appears genuinely testable — should get our_verdict
issues.description: From ISS-703-sha256-forensics.c2.json: PRED-TOROID-003 (Schumann mode splitting Δf ≈ 0.336 Hz) is currently unclassified (our_verdict=null). The curmudgeon notes this is genuinely novel — the toroidal cavity geometry implies different mode structure from a spherical cavity, and the predicted frequency split is quantitatively specific and testable. It should be reclassified as testable_prediction or similar, not left null alongside retrospective citations.

issues.title: SEC-2b.7 W001 7-sigma punch line conflates sigma_distance (deviation from zero) with sigma_residual (deviation from dome prediction)
issues.description: From part2b-W001-signflip.c2.json (reviewed 2026-04-12): The central argument — "A 7-sigma deviation with the wrong sign is not a non-detection — it is a detection of the wrong answer" — conflates two different metrics. sigma_distance (7.3) measures deviation from zero (no effect); it says nothing about which direction the dome predicts. A dome that predicts a positive effect and sees a 7-sigma positive result would also have sigma_distance = 7.3. The "wrong sign" claim requires comparing to what the dome actually predicted, not to zero. The paragraph needs to state the dome-predicted direction and show the observation contradicts it.

issues.title: SEC-7.2 (old SEC-4.9): Refraction WINs count claim partially unsupported — only WIN-016 and WIN-017 explicitly cite aetheric refraction in detail text
issues.description: From SEC-4.9.json (reviewed 2026-04-06, staleness-uncertain — verify before deep investigation): The section claims "at least six WINs rely on aetheric refraction" (WIN-016, 017, 026, 033, 056, 065), but only WIN-016 and WIN-017 explicitly invoke n(r) in their detail_evidence. WIN-026, WIN-033, WIN-056, WIN-065 use refraction only implicitly or not at all. The count should be verified against current WIN text before asserting six.
issues.staleness_note: Source review was cycle 1, dated 2026-04-06. sections.json has 89 commits since then. Verify finding still applies before deep investigation.

issues.title: SEC-2b.1 stratification: hardcoded count "Twelve are misleading" stale (actual: 22)
issues.description: SEC-2b.1 stratification paragraph says "Twelve are misleading (duplicated, circular, or non-discriminating)." Current wins.json has 22 WINs with verdict=Misleading and all have monitoring=none. Hardcoded 12 is stale. Note: this is separate from the EXP-103 scope (which covers SC count); this is the Misleading count.

issues.title: SEC-2b.1 accuracy arithmetic stale: 67 WINs and 5 uncounted-failures need updating
issues.description: SEC-2b.1 uses "67/(67+4+5) ≈ 88.2%" for dome accuracy arithmetic. The dome now claims 69 WINs (not 67). uncounted-failures.json has 8 entries (not 5). Both hardcoded numbers are stale. This is in the "monitoring dashboard accuracy" paragraph.

issues.title: WIN-058 verdict: 'Misleading' may not fit — not cherry-picked/duplicated/circular
issues.description: HOLISTIC-HOL-TAXONOMY C2: WIN-058 (Unified angular coordinate θ = −lonE) verdict is Misleading but the finding is not cherry-picking/duplicating/circularity — it's that the dome fixed a broken proxy and claims it as a discovery. A more accurate verdict might be "Not Demonstrated" (no claim beyond coordinate definition) or "Std Model Explains" (it just IS geographic longitude). Needs human adjudication.

issues.title: SEC-3.5.1: B(r) formula uses single-variable r instead of r_N/r_S; κ coupling constant attributed to wrong storms
issues.description: Two major errors in Section 3.5.1: (1) B(r) formula written as 62,376×e^(−r/8619) + 64,852×e^(−r/8619) using single variable r for both terms, then incorrectly claims this simplifies to 127,228. The correct formula uses r_N and r_S (distance from North/South pole respectively) — these are not the same and do not sum to 127,228. (2) κ coupling constant 10.9 nT attributed to "2003 Halloween storm" but dome WIN-012 explicitly states this comes from BOU 2017 solar eclipse (10.9 nT) and 1997 Mohe eclipse (6.5 μGal) — different events, different physics. Both are factual errors requiring prose rewrite.
issues.suggested_fix: Analyst Mode 1: (a) Fix B(r) formula to use r_N and r_S, remove incorrect simplification claim, make correct argument about shared decay constants. (b) Fix κ attribution from Halloween storm to BOU 2017 + Mohe 1997 eclipses per WIN-012. Cross-check with sections 3.5.8 which already uses correct dual-variable formula.

issues.title: WIN-010 verdict-text mismatch: verdict=Self-Contradicted but all text says Non-discriminating
issues.description: WIN-010 verdict was flipped to Self-Contradicted (EXP-078, approved 2026-04-12) but detail_evidence, detail_verdict_text, finding, tldr_evidence, and tldr_verdict were NOT updated. Text still argues Chapman Non-discriminating. Reader sees verdict=Self-Contradicted with evidence for a completely different verdict category. Per WIN-010.c3 curmudgeon review (cycle 3, conf 0.95). The new self-contradiction argument is: eclipse geometry requires definite solar altitude, which varies 83% seasonally due to n(r), making dome eclipse predictions indeterminate. Analyst must (1) rewrite detail_verdict_text to explain self-contradiction, (2) update finding field, (3) update both TLDRs. Also: fix duplicated paragraph in detail_evidence (duplicate starts with "Note: the dome model's own eclipse validation").

issues.title: SEC-1.7 hardcodes stale WIN count (67) and stale 49% gravity fraction
issues.description: Section 1.7 (Gravity Paradox, in part1 HTML) says "across the dome's 67 WINs" but dome now has 69 WINs. Also "Gravity Pervades 49% of the Model's Predictions" heading: 33/67=49% was correct for 67 WINs but 33/70≠49% (= 47%). Analyst must: (1) count how many of the new WINs (WIN-068 and any others added) involve gravity to get the correct numerator, (2) update the 67 count to current dome WIN count, (3) recalculate the 49% fraction and update the heading if changed. From SEC-1.7.c1 curmudgeon review (reviewed 2026-04-12T21:15:00Z).

issues.title: SEC-4.5.2 cross-reference error: WIN-045/046/049/050/051 reference wrong section number
issues.description: SEC-4.5.2 curmudgeon review found: (1) WIN-045/046/049/050/051 WIN text may reference stale section numbers but current wins.json shows Section 2.2 (correct post-V6). (2) DOCX/HTML section numbering mismatch: HTML section is 2.2 but DOCX uses 4.5.4 numbering, creating inconsistency between formats. This is the same HTML/DOCX mismatch pattern as ISS-806 for SEC-4.5.3. Also flagged: tidal force scaling uses 1/r² (gravitational force) when tidal differential force should be 1/r³ — may need clarification note in section prose. Staleness note: WIN cross-refs appear already updated to 2.2; DOCX number mismatch is the live issue.
issues.staleness_note: WIN references appear already fixed in wins.json (say Section 2.2 linking to #part2). Focus on DOCX section numbering.
